message:
fix(indexer/lexical): sharded rebuild also persists equivalence ledger + generation manifest

Two rebuild paths exist for the tantivy-on-SQLite lexical index:

  1. `rebuild_tantivy_from_db_with_options` — in-place, single-writer
     rebuild over the DB in small batches (the common small-corpus
     path).
  2. `rebuild_tantivy_from_db_via_staged_shards` — parallel shard-based
     rebuild for large corpora (many tens of thousands of
     conversations) that needs multiple writer threads to finish in
     reasonable wall-clock time.

Path 1 published two durability artifacts on successful rebuild:
  - the equivalence-evidence ledger (manifest fingerprint + golden
    query hit counts, so a downstream consumer can prove the new index
    is semantically equivalent to the authoritative DB state), and
  - the lexical generation manifest (Built → Validated → Published,
    carrying conversation_count / message_count / indexed_doc_count /
    equivalence_manifest_fingerprint).

Path 2 had been shipping an incomplete outcome: no equivalence ledger
was written, and the generation manifest wasn't published. The shard
merge ran an `equivalence_accumulator` but discarded it (the
`LexicalRebuildOutcome` it returned had `equivalence: None`). That
meant large-corpus rebuilds — the rebuilds we most want an audit
trail for — produced an index with no provenance record at all.

Refactor + fix in one pass:

  - Extract the shared post-rebuild publish logic into a new private
    helper `persist_lexical_rebuild_publish_artifacts(...)` that takes
    `index_path`, `rebuild_state`, counts, and the finalized
    `equivalence_evidence`. It handles the equivalence-info log,
    writes the evidence ledger, constructs the
    `LexicalGenerationManifest` with Built → Validated → Published
    transitions, and stores it with the `.with_context(...)` error
    wrapping for a self-describing failure.

  - Path 1 (`rebuild_tantivy_from_db_with_options`) now calls the
    shared helper instead of inlining the same ~60 lines. Identical
    behavior; pure refactor on that side.

  - Path 2 (`rebuild_tantivy_from_db_via_staged_shards`) now
    introduces `let mut equivalence_accumulator =
    LexicalRebuildEquivalenceAccumulator::new()` at the top of the
    shard loop, feeds each shard-produced packet through
    `equivalence_accumulator.absorb_packet(&packet)` as it's produced,
    then finalizes + calls the shared helper at end-of-rebuild. The
    returned outcome now correctly surfaces
    `equivalence: Some(equivalence_evidence)` — matching path 1 and
    making the equivalence field authoritative across both rebuild
    paths.

No changes to the equivalence-evidence format or the manifest shape.
This is purely "the shard-parallel rebuild now writes the same
provenance records the single-writer rebuild already did."

Downstream consumers that branch on `outcome.equivalence.is_some()`
(e.g. CI drift checks) will now get the Some branch for shard
rebuilds too, which is the intended behavior and matches what the
LexicalRebuildOutcome doc-comment claimed but the code didn't deliver.
